source.title: Encoding of conditioned fear in central amygdala inhibitory circuits

confidence_rationale: This Nature paper provides the single-unit and optogenetic-level characterisation of CeA inhibitory microcircuits as a fear acquisition gate and fear expression driver — directly relevant to CeAAnalog's architecture in SD-035. The dissociation between CEl (lateral subdivision: acquisition-gated, phasic) and CEm (medial subdivision: output neurons driving conditioned responses) maps to CeAAnalog's internal structure: fast-prime onset (CEl-like phasic acquisition gate) and mode_prior output (CEm-like sustained output). The GABAergic inhibitory gating mechanism within CeA supports the claim that CeA functions as a mode-prior emitter with internal gating rather than a simple relay.
failure_signatures: If CeA activity during fear acquisition required direct input from BLA on every trial (not just during initial conditioning), the semi-autonomous CeA mode-prior function in SD-035 would be underspecified.

The CEl-CEm dissociation implies CeAAnalog needs internal inhibitory gating logic; if this gating is absent in the non-trainable initial pass, MECH-046 validation may fail because mode_prior dynamics are too simple.

mapping.source_claim_statement: CEl neurons are required for fear acquisition (phasic, acquisition-gated); CEm neurons drive conditioned fear responses (output relay). Cell-type-specific plasticity of phasic/tonic activity in the CEl-to-CEm pathway gates fear expression and regulates fear generalization.
mapping.ree_translation: SD-035's CeAAnalog emits mode_prior pre-softmax to SalienceCoordinator (CEm-like sustained output) and fast_prime as a phasic conditioned-cue signal (CEl-like acquisition-gated). The phasic/tonic dissociation documented by Ciocchi directly supports CeAAnalog's theta_cea_fast threshold architecture: fast_prime fires for conditioned harm cues (CEl-like) while mode_prior provides the sustained motivational bias (CEm-like). The GABAergic inhibitory basis supports CeAAnalog's mode_prior_log_odds operating as an additive pre-softmax log-odds bias (not a direct activating signal).
mapping.mapping_caveat: Study uses fear conditioning in mice; SD-035 covers harm avoidance more broadly. CeA subdivision anatomy (CEl/CEm) is not explicitly modelled in SD-035's initial non-trainable pass — the CEl-CEm interneuron circuit maps only loosely onto fast_prime vs mode_prior as separate output channels.
mapping.source_context: In vivo electrophysiology, optogenetics in C57BL/6 mice; auditory fear conditioning; Nature paper

confidence_components.notes: Highest venue (Nature), optogenetic circuit dissection, clear subdivision resolution. Mapping fidelity limited because SD-035's initial non-trainable pass does not implement CEl/CEm distinction explicitly.
